behavior_summary: BLANKETS::look is the 'U8P_BLANKETS::look' (examine) handler for blanket-type items. When a player (or relevant actor/process) examines the item, the script queries the item's frame/state and, if the frame equals 0, 1, or 2, it emits the short description "blanket " using the item's bark method and then suspends the process.

Frames 0, 1, and 2 are treated identically: the script builds or references the string "blanket ", calls Item::bark with the item, that string, and the current pid context, frees the temporary string, and runs an implies/pop/suspend control sequence to pause the process after barking.
If the item's frame is any other value the handler performs no special action and returns normally.
notable_control_flow: Entry occurs on the 'U8P_BLANKETS::look' event for a BLANKETS instance (examine trigger).
Control branches on Item::getFrame(): three explicit branches check for frame == 0, == 1, and == 2. Each matching branch performs the same bark and suspend sequence.

After calling Item::bark the script frees the temporary string and uses an implies/pop/suspend sequence (engine control idiom) to suspend the process — indicating the message was displayed and handling/dialog state is paused.
Non-matching frames fall through with no side effects.
open_questions: What semantic difference (if any) exists between frames 0, 1, and 2 beyond visual variants? The script treats them identically, but art/animation differences are possible.
Precisely where the pid (process/player id) value is sourced from in the caller context — it's read and passed to Item::bark but its origin is inferred from calling convention.
Why the script uses the implies/pop/suspend sequence here instead of a simple return — is it coordinating with a broader dialog/processing system or preventing immediate re-entry?

main_function.name: U8P_BLANKETS::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an examine ('U8P_BLANKETS::look') event for blanket items by identifying the item to the viewer. It queries the item's visual frame and, for frames 0, 1, or 2, instructs the item to 'bark' the label "blanket " to the viewer and then suspends further processing of the message.
entry_conditions: Triggered by Event 0 (the actor/player looks at or examines the BLANKETS item).
Runs with an item/object pointer parameter (inferred at BP+06) representing the specific blanket instance.

function_map.name: U8P_BLANKETS::look
function_map.role: Examine ('U8P_BLANKETS::look') event handler for BLANKETS items

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Engine/item API: return numeric frame/state of the item used to branch behavior

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item API: emit/display a short text message from the item to the provided process/player context

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the item's numeric frame/state so the handler can determine whether to describe it as a blanket (frame comparison against 0,1,2).

Cause the item to display/say the short string "blanket " to the viewer/process context (pid). The call passes the item, the string, and the pid; the returned value is used in the subsequent implies/pop/suspend sequence.

reads.name: pid
reads.likely_effect: Provides the process/player context passed to Item::bark so the message targets the correct viewer/process.
